summary: v1.5 drip campaign tested end-to-end. Backend: 33/33 pytest tests pass (8 new drip tests + 17 auth/admin regression + 8 v1.2 packages/leads/checkout). Frontend: full E2E flow works — lead captured via /calculadora → admin login → tab-drip renders 4 KPI cards + queue table → drip-fire button transitions row status pending→sent → drip-run-now polls the queue. Brute-force lockout (previous iteration's HIGH-priority bug) is now confirmed FIXED — 429 fires reliably after 5 wrong attempts (X-Forwarded-For fix works).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py cancel_drip_for_email is only invoked inside /api/checkout/status transition-to-paid branch. Consider also invoking on webhook (POST /api/webhook/stripe) so that if Stripe pushes the paid event before the client polls /checkout/status, the drip is still cancelled promptly. Currently the webhook only updates payment_transactions, not drip.

drip_service.send_due_emails: individual template failures caught only for missing template. If send_email throws, exception propagates and loop breaks — wrap in try/except per doc to keep other due emails from being blocked.
server.py:339 uses helper timedelta_days defined at line 402 — works due to hoisting but is ugly; use datetime.timedelta directly.
drip_service.tpl_day1 has confusing `.replace('{:,.0f}', '')` chain that does nothing on f-string output. Cosmetic only.
DRIP_SCHEDULE delays are hardcoded — fine for prod but no easy way to shorten for staging; consider env override.
test_credentials: wesleynb10@gmail.com / FinPremium2026! (admin) — Resend test-mode only delivers to this address
seed_data_creation: Test lead emails prefixed TEST_ (drip, buyer, delays) plus e2edrip{ts}@example.com from browser flow. All persisted in mongo — cleanup optional.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v1.5 drip fully green. If future iterations extend the sequence, verify DRIP_SCHEDULE length matches TEMPLATES map (currently 5 steps). Cancellation flow is tested via direct cancel_drip_for_email() call because Stripe payments cannot be actually confirmed in test — the /checkout/status codepath that calls it is trivially visible in server.py:270.
rca of the issue: N/A — no unresolved bugs this iteration. Previous v1.4 brute-force lockout bug is verified fixed.
